page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 128 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core testing and Passmark CPU scores. It also demonstrates exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations, reaching 113.83 million operations per second, and static web serving at 104.85 GB/sec. The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate workloads like cryptography, software compilation, and vector calculations.

This hardware profile is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference. While prompt processing for 7B models is highly efficient at 303.91 tokens per second, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for large-scale model training or high-parameter text generation. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a deliberate tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute capacity and memory speed over massive memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, driving top-tier performance in software compilation, Redis operations, and static web serving. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core execution and efficient medium-scale LLM inference, though the 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio limits memory-heavy tasks. This instance is highly efficient for compute-bound workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor, delivering top-tier multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and DDR5 memory for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
Top-tier performance in software compilation, Redis, and static web serving
Optimized for compute-bound workloads, databases, and medium-scale LLM inference
